title: neopanax laetus
intro: Neopanax laetus, also known as the kawakawa tree or New Zealand pepper tree, is a plant species native to New Zealand and parts of the Pacific Islands. The leaves and seeds of the Neopanax laetus plant have been used in traditional medicine for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant's medicinal properties include pain relief, wound healing, and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to various health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound recovery, and enhanced immune function. Neopanax laetus is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil, and its herbal preparations have also been used in topical creams and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
